title: Success
content:
I'd rather be a Could Be
        If I could not be an Are;
     For a Could Be is a May Be,
        With a chance of touching par.
     I'd rather be a Has Been
        Than a Might Have Been, by far;
     For a Might Have Been has never been,
        But a Has was once an Are.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Success", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
